comments.author: @MrMnohar
comments.text:
THIS FILM HERE IS ONLY ONE SIDE OF GUYANA 🇬🇾. 
GUYANA 🇬🇾 IS VERY MUCH MORE THAN THAT WITH ITS DEVELOPMENTS  & INFRASTRUCTURES.
We the people of GUYANA 🇬🇾 are the  most friendliness  people here on earth....

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: @eulexy3496 Thank you for sharing your memories. It's fascinating to hear from someone who experienced Georgetown decades ago. Finding the balance between modernization and preserving a country's unique character is never easy. I really appreciate your thoughtful perspective.

replies.author: @eulexy3496
replies.text: I lived in Georgetown, the capital of Guyana, in the 1980's, and now see  --  and hear from friends  - -  how "modernization" has set in, making the once beautiful city ( by American officials called "garden city") , beautiful despite its infrastructure problems , into a place with modern bridges (replacing the floating bridge over the Demerara), with steel and  glass hotels that need air conditioning, while the traditional buildings, as the university, are so cool with their natural air ventilation that one almost feels to cold despite 34C /94F degrees outside. I wished Guyana would preserve more of its special atmosphere, the feel of being there, that I loved, and experienced as unlike from any other country around.....

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective. This film highlights just one aspect of Guyana, and I truly appreciate you pointing out the country’s growth and the warmth of its people.

comments.author: @LindaPersaud-e1c
comments.text: this cannot be the same country of my birth . I laughed at this documentary because although i recognise some of what is described most of it applies to the interior of the country and excludes a developing country that  is much more developed. Guyana  is more than its indigenous people, its rainforests etc , it is a nation that is striving to develop and has a rich heritage of seven races, colonial history, independence, becoming a republic nd is still marching on into the 21st century and suceeding.May the land of many waters be blessed.

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective and for highlighting these important aspects of Guyana’s identity and progress. I’m glad the film sparked this conversation, and I truly appreciate your insight into the rich history and diversity of your country.

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: Absolutely stunning! Guyana’s landscapes look like a world forgotten by time. It’s incredible to see how these ancient Tepuis have remained untouched for billions of years.

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: I’m glad you enjoyed the journey through Guyana’s timeless landscapes.

comments.author: @BLACKBANG592
comments.text: Guyana is going to be  a Hindu country.

replies.author: @eulexy3496
replies.text: So you want to do an ethnic cleansing, murdering every other tribe ??? It is cultural diversity that makes Guyana what it is.

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: You’re right! Nearly 25% of Guyana’s population is of Indian descent — brought by the British as indentured laborers after slavery was abolished. Hinduism is already one of the country’s major religions. A fascinating mix of cultures in the middle of the Amazon. 🌿
